description: Extended Typestate preset collection. Domain-specific machines for IFRS 17, IFRS 9, MCMC, and Bayesian analysis. Designed for teaching complex systems to new audiences.

ifrs17_reinsurance.label: IFRS 17: Reinsurance Held
ifrs17_reinsurance.description: Reinsurance contract from ceding company perspective. Mirrors direct insurance lifecycle but with net cost recovery logic and different gain/loss recognition timing.

ifrs17_investmentComponent.label: IFRS 17: Investment Component
ifrs17_investmentComponent.description: Determining whether a contract has a distinct investment component that must be separated. If distinct, it is excluded from insurance revenue and measured under IFRS 9.

ifrs17_riskAdjustment.label: IFRS 17: Risk Adjustment
ifrs17_riskAdjustment.description: The risk adjustment for non-financial risk compensates for uncertainty in cash flows. Computed each period, released as risk expires, and re-estimated on new information.

ifrs17_transition.label: IFRS 17: Transition Approaches
ifrs17_transition.description: First-time adoption of IFRS 17: choose between full retrospective (ideal but often impracticable), modified retrospective, or fair value approach.

ifrs17_contractBoundary.label: IFRS 17: Contract Boundary
ifrs17_contractBoundary.description: Determining which future cash flows belong within the contract boundary. Cash flows outside the boundary are treated as future contracts.

ifrs17_revenueRecog.label: IFRS 17: Revenue Recognition
ifrs17_revenueRecog.description: Insurance revenue is recognized as coverage units are provided, not when premium is received. Revenue = expected claims + risk adjustment release + CSM release.

ifrs17_annualCohorts.label: IFRS 17: Annual Cohort Grouping
ifrs17_annualCohorts.description: Contracts must be grouped into annual cohorts (issued within one year). Within each cohort, three profitability groups: onerous, no significant risk of becoming onerous, and remaining.

ifrs17_discountRate.label: IFRS 17: Discount Rate
ifrs17_discountRate.description: Two approaches to determining discount rates: bottom-up (risk-free + illiquidity premium) and top-down (asset yield - credit risk). Both should produce equivalent results.

ifrs17_claimsDev.label: IFRS 17: Claims Development
ifrs17_claimsDev.description: Incurred claims lifecycle from event occurrence through reporting, reserving, adjustment, and settlement. The Liability for Incurred Claims (LIC) tracks this.

ifrs17_experienceAdj.label: IFRS 17: Experience Adjustments
ifrs17_experienceAdj.description: Differences between expected and actual cash flows. Premium experience adjustments adjust revenue. Claims experience adjustments adjust the fulfilment cash flows.

ifrs17_presentation.label: IFRS 17: P&L Presentation
ifrs17_presentation.description: How insurance results flow through the income statement: insurance revenue, insurance service expense, insurance finance income/expense.

ifrs17_interimReport.label: IFRS 17: Interim Reporting
ifrs17_interimReport.description: Handling interim (quarterly) vs annual reporting. Interim periods use estimates that may be trued-up at year-end. Locked-in rates and experience adjustments interact differently at interim.

ifrs9_loanModification.label: IFRS 9: Loan Modification
ifrs9_loanModification.description: When loan terms change: assess whether modification is substantial (derecognize and recognize new) or non-substantial (adjust carrying amount). The 10% test on present value of cash flows is the key threshold.

ifrs9_derecognition.label: IFRS 9: Derecognition
ifrs9_derecognition.description: The derecognition decision tree: have contractual rights expired? If not, has the entity transferred substantially all risks and rewards? If ambiguous, has it retained control?

ifrs9_pdModel.label: IFRS 9: PD Model Lifecycle
ifrs9_pdModel.description: Probability of Default model from development through validation, deployment, monitoring, and eventual recalibration or retirement.

ifrs9_forwardLooking.label: IFRS 9: Forward-Looking Overlay
ifrs9_forwardLooking.description: Incorporating forward-looking macroeconomic information into ECL. Define scenarios, assign probabilities, compute scenario-weighted ECL, apply management overlay if needed.

ifrs9_day1PL.label: IFRS 9: Day-1 Profit/Loss
ifrs9_day1PL.description: When fair value at initial recognition differs from transaction price. Day-1 gains are recognized only if fair value is evidenced by observable market data. Otherwise deferred.

ifrs9_poci.label: IFRS 9: POCI Instruments
ifrs9_poci.description: Purchased or originated credit-impaired instruments. These start in Stage 3 equivalent but use credit-adjusted EIR. Only cumulative changes in lifetime ECL after purchase are recognized.

ifrs9_collectiveAssessment.label: IFRS 9: Collective vs Individual
ifrs9_collectiveAssessment.description: Deciding between individual and collective impairment assessment. Large exposures get individual assessment. Homogeneous pools use collective models. Some get both.

ifrs9_scenarioWeighting.label: IFRS 9: Macro Scenario Weighting
ifrs9_scenarioWeighting.description: ECL must reflect probability-weighted outcomes. Typically 3-5 scenarios (base, optimistic, downturn, severe) with expert-assigned probabilities summing to 1.

ifrs9_eirMethod.label: IFRS 9: Effective Interest Rate
ifrs9_eirMethod.description: EIR method: compute rate that discounts future cash flows to carrying amount. Apply each period. Handle modifications by recalculating or using original EIR.

ifrs9_reclassification.label: IFRS 9: Reclassification
ifrs9_reclassification.description: Reclassification between measurement categories is permitted only when the entity changes its business model. Reclassification is applied prospectively from the reclassification date.

ifrs9_financialGuarantee.label: IFRS 9: Financial Guarantee
ifrs9_financialGuarantee.description: Financial guarantee contract lifecycle: initial recognition at fair value, subsequent measurement at higher of ECL provision and amortized premium, through to expiry or payout.

ifrs9_writeoffRecovery.label: IFRS 9: Write-off and Recovery
ifrs9_writeoffRecovery.description: After write-off, the entity may still pursue recovery. Amounts recovered are recognized in P&L. The asset remains derecognized even if partial recovery occurs.

mcmc_hmc.label: Hamiltonian Monte Carlo
mcmc_hmc.description: HMC augments the parameter space with momentum variables. Leapfrog integration simulates Hamiltonian dynamics to propose distant points with high acceptance probability.

mcmc_nuts.label: No-U-Turn Sampler (NUTS)
mcmc_nuts.description: NUTS automatically tunes the trajectory length in HMC by building a binary tree and stopping when the trajectory starts to double back (U-turn criterion).

mcmc_parallelTempering.label: Parallel Tempering
mcmc_parallelTempering.description: Run multiple chains at different temperatures. Hot chains explore freely, cold chains sample accurately. Periodically swap states between adjacent temperatures based on Metropolis criterion.

mcmc_sliceSampling.label: Slice Sampling
mcmc_sliceSampling.description: Slice sampling introduces an auxiliary variable to define a horizontal slice under the density. Then uniformly samples from the slice. Automatically adapts step size.

mcmc_rjmcmc.label: Reversible Jump MCMC
mcmc_rjmcmc.description: RJMCMC allows moves between models of different dimensionality. Used for model selection where the number of parameters is unknown (e.g., number of mixture components).

mcmc_adaptive.label: Adaptive MCMC
mcmc_adaptive.description: Automatically tunes the proposal distribution during warmup using the sample covariance of accepted points. Adaptation is disabled after warmup to preserve ergodicity.

mcmc_multiChain.label: Multi-Chain Management
mcmc_multiChain.description: Running multiple independent chains, monitoring inter-chain variance (R-hat), and combining results. Chains that diverge are flagged and potentially restarted.

mcmc_thinning.label: MCMC Post-Processing
mcmc_thinning.description: After sampling: discard warmup, thin to reduce autocorrelation, compute summaries, check effective sample size, and store results.

mcmc_ppc.label: Posterior Predictive Check
mcmc_ppc.description: Generate simulated data from the posterior predictive distribution. Compare to observed data using test statistics. Systematic discrepancies indicate model misspecification.

mcmc_smc.label: Sequential Monte Carlo
mcmc_smc.description: Particle filter: maintain a weighted set of particles, update weights as new data arrives, resample when effective sample size drops. Used for online/streaming inference.

mcmc_diagnosticSuite.label: MCMC Diagnostic Suite
mcmc_diagnosticSuite.description: Comprehensive diagnostic workflow: trace plots, autocorrelation, R-hat, ESS, divergence checks, energy diagnostics. Each check can pass or flag issues.

bayes_hierarchical.label: Bayesian Hierarchical Model
bayes_hierarchical.description: Multi-level model: hyperpriors govern group-level parameters, which govern unit-level parameters. Partial pooling emerges naturally. Fit via MCMC or variational inference.

bayes_optimization.label: Bayesian Optimization
bayes_optimization.description: Black-box optimization: fit a Gaussian Process surrogate to observations, maximize an acquisition function (EI, UCB) to select the next evaluation point, observe, update, repeat.

bayes_variational.label: Variational Inference (ELBO)
bayes_variational.description: Approximate the posterior with a simpler distribution by maximizing the Evidence Lower Bound (ELBO). Faster than MCMC but introduces approximation error.

bayes_priorElicitation.label: Prior Elicitation
bayes_priorElicitation.description: Structured process for translating expert knowledge into prior distributions. Elicit quantiles, check consistency, fit parametric family, validate with prior predictive simulation.

bayes_clinicalTrial.label: Bayesian Clinical Trial
bayes_clinicalTrial.description: Adaptive Bayesian trial: update treatment effect posterior after each cohort. Allow early stopping for efficacy, futility, or safety. Adjust randomization ratios based on interim data.

bayes_changepoint.label: Bayesian Changepoint Detection
bayes_changepoint.description: Online changepoint detection: maintain run-length posterior. At each observation, either the current regime continues or a changepoint occurred. Compute posterior over changepoint locations.

bayes_networkLearning.label: Bayesian Network Learning
bayes_networkLearning.description: Learning both structure and parameters of a Bayesian network from data. Structure search (score-based or constraint-based), then parameter estimation given the learned DAG.

bayes_empirical.label: Empirical Bayes
bayes_empirical.description: Two-stage estimation: first estimate hyperparameters from the marginal likelihood (the empirical step), then use those as fixed priors for the full Bayesian analysis.

bayes_metaAnalysis.label: Bayesian Meta-Analysis
bayes_metaAnalysis.description: Combining effect sizes from multiple studies. Random-effects model accounts for between-study heterogeneity. The posterior on the overall effect incorporates uncertainty from all sources.

bayes_decisionTheory.label: Bayesian Decision Theory
bayes_decisionTheory.description: Optimal decision-making under uncertainty. Define a loss function, compute posterior expected loss for each action, choose the action minimizing expected loss (Bayes risk).

bayes_timeSeries.label: Bayesian State Space Model
bayes_timeSeries.description: State space model with Bayesian inference: hidden state evolves via transition equation, observations generated via observation equation. Kalman filter (linear) or particle filter (nonlinear).

bayes_nonparametrics.label: Bayesian Nonparametrics (DPM)
bayes_nonparametrics.description: Dirichlet Process Mixture: the number of clusters is inferred from data. New observations can join existing clusters or create new ones. The posterior over partitions is updated via Chinese Restaurant Process or stick-breaking.
